category_title: Struggling readers who use physical movement and gestures to participate
student_description: Luis is an eager student who relies heavily on physical movement, rhythm, and memory rather than text-based decoding. He frequently guesses words based on illustrations or reads slightly behind his peers' voices. He uniquely requires explicit phonemic segmentation, rhythmic reading structures, and carefully leveled texts to prevent cognitive overload and peer frustration.

criteria.criterion_name: Integration of Tangible and Home Literacies
criteria.rationale: Luis's physical and out-of-school literacy practices mediate his classroom participation, so connecting tangible home items to school tasks validates his learning style.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes structured opportunities (e.g., 'The Important Bag') for Luis to bring in and discuss physical items from home to bridge his out-of-school and in-school literacies.

criteria.rationale: Because unmanageable scripts lead to forgotten words and peer frustration, Luis requires ongoing assessment to ensure texts match his evolving abilities.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes a mechanism for continuously assessing Luis's decoding abilities to ensure assigned reading parts remain manageable and appropriately leveled over time.
